targets.label: RDNA
targets.description: AMD RDNA benchmark node results collected over SSH and compared against llama.cpp on the same hardware.

methodology.benchmark_style: remote repeated multi-scenario runs over SSH with per-model same-machine llama.cpp baselines
notes: ZINC runs use the remote ReleaseFast build with RADV_PERFTEST=coop_matrix.
llama.cpp comparison runs on the RDNA node against the same model file, preferably through one per-model llama-server launch reused across the scenario matrix.
Each model runs a scenario matrix: core prompt, medium context, long context, and extended decode.
Use --all-models to auto-discover GGUFs on the RDNA node instead of the default reference set.
If remote llama-server is unavailable, the suite falls back to remote llama-cli for baseline collection.
The suite assumes the remote node is already in a clean benchmark state unless --rdna-sync / --rdna-build / --rdna-start-llama are provided.

scenarios.label: Long Decode
scenarios.prompt: Write six short bullet points explaining why local LLM benchmark reports should separate prefill throughput from decode throughput.
notes: Longer generation target instead of a short factual completion

zinc.name: ZINC
zinc.output_preview:
1. **Distinct Performance Metrics**: Prefill throughput and decode throughput measure different aspects of LLM performance. Prefill throughput focuses on the speed of generating the initial context, while decode throughput evaluates the speed of generating subsequent tokens. Separating these metrics provides a clearer understanding of the model's overall performance.

2. **Model Optimization**: By separating prefill and decode throughput, developers can identify specific bottlenecks in the model's architecture or implementation. This allows for targeted

targets.label: Metal
targets.description: Apple Silicon local benchmark suite rendered from the current workspace build.

methodology.benchmark_style: multi-scenario repeated CLI runs with same-model local llama.cpp comparison
notes: ZINC is measured from the local ReleaseFast build in the current workspace.
Each model runs a scenario matrix: core prompt, medium context, long context, and extended decode.
Gemma and GPT-OSS cases use chat-template mode; Qwen cases use raw completion mode to avoid visible thinking scaffolds in CLI output.
Default Metal reference cases resolve from the managed model cache in ~/Library/Caches/zinc/models/models.
Use --metal-pull-missing to install missing managed models through `zinc model pull` before the suite runs.
When available, llama.cpp is measured from one local llama-server launch per model so the same loaded model can serve the whole scenario matrix.
If llama-server is unavailable, the suite falls back to local llama-cli for same-model CLI comparison.
